item.title: George Harrison
item.description: Instrument: Lead guitar, slide guitar, sitar, vocals Genres: Rock, pop rock, folk rock Bands: The Beatles, Traveling Wilburys Era: British Invasion (with the Beatles, 1963–1970); solo, 1968–2002 RYM · Apple Music · Spotify George Harrison played as if notes cost money.

item.title: John Lennon
item.description: Instrument: Rhythm guitar, vocals, piano, harmonica Genres: Rock, singer-songwriter, pop rock Bands: The Beatles, Plastic Ono Band Era: British Invasion (with the Beatles, 1963–1970); solo, 1968–1980 RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The most influential rock voice of its generation belonged to a man who ...

item.title: George Martin
item.description: Era: 1950s–1998 (primary period: 1962–1970) Genres: Pop rock, Merseybeat, psychedelic pop, art rock, baroque pop Key artists: The Beatles RYM · Apple Music · Spotify George Martin signed the Beatles because nobody else would, and the records they made together over the next eight years changed what ...

item.title: Phil Spector
item.description: Era: Late 1950s–1970s (primary period: 1961–1966) Genres: Girl group, Pop soul, Rock & roll, pop rock Key artists: The Ronettes, The Crystals, The Righteous Brothers, The Beatles, Ike & Tina Turner RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Phil Spector is the first pop producer to matter as an auteur, the...

item.title: Paul McCartney
item.description: Instrument: Bass, vocals, piano, guitar, drums Genres: Rock, pop rock, pop Bands: The Beatles, Wings, the Fireman Era: British Invasion (with the Beatles, 1963–1970); solo, 1970–present RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Paul McCartney plays the bass like a second singer.

item.title: The Funk Brothers
item.description: Location: Hitsville U.S.A., Detroit, Michigan Period: 1959–1972 Key members: James Jamerson (bass), Benny Benjamin (drums), Earl Van Dyke (keyboards), Robert White (guitar), Eddie Willis (guitar), Joe Messina (guitar), Jack Ashford (percussion), Uriel Jones (drums) RYM The most prolific and least cr...

item.title: Lennon-McCartney
item.description: Era: 1957–1970 Genres: Pop rock, Merseybeat, psychedelic pop, art rock Key collaborators: George Martin (producer), The Beatles (performing vehicle) RYM (Lennon) · RYM (McCartney) · Apple Music (Lennon) · Apple Music (McCartney) · Spotify (Lennon) · Spotify (McCartney) John Lennon and Paul McCartney...

item.title: The Beach Boys
item.description: Genres: Surf rock, vocal surf, baroque pop, sunshine pop, psychedelic pop, progressive pop, pop rock Scene: Hawthorne / Hollywood, California Era: 1961–present (primary period: 1963–1970) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The Beach Boys are one instrument made of five throats.

item.title: The Beatles
item.description: Genres: Pop rock, psychedelic pop, art rock Scene: Merseybeat Movement: British beat boom Era: British Invasion (1963–1970) Playlist: FAB RYM · Apple Music · Spotify One chord opens A Hard Day’s Night, and no two musicians have ever agreed how to name it.

item.title: The Rolling Stones
item.description: Genres: British blues, blues rock, rock & roll, roots rock Scene: London (Richmond, then Soho) Era: 1962–present (primary period: 1968–1972) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Keith Richards plays behind the beat.
